channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A surprise resignation could open the door for an independent to win a Montana Senate seat
item.description: Two-term GOP Sen. Steve Daines shocked Montana when he announced his retirement. Democrats worry a new independent candidate will split their party's vote.
item.content:encoded:
Two-term GOP Sen. Steve Daines shocked Montana when he announced his retirement. Democrats worry a new independent candidate will split their party's vote.

(Image credit: Kirk Siegler)
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: The depleted Education Department will move out of its headquarters
item.description: In August, Education Department employees will relocate to a smaller office roughly a block away, and the larger Energy Department will take over the old headquarters.
item.content:encoded:
In August, Education Department employees will relocate to a smaller office roughly a block away, and the larger Energy Department will take over the old headquarters.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump administration's Anthropic ban
item.description: The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."
item.content:encoded:
The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."

(Image credit: RICCARDO MILANI/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order to pay TSA agents as Congress struggles to reach funding deal
item.description: It's an extraordinary move that came as senators were reviewing a "last and final" offer to end the funding impasse that has jammed airports and disrupted travel, just as TSA workers faced another missed paycheck Friday.
item.content:encoded:
It's an extraordinary move that came as senators were reviewing a "last and final" offer to end the funding impasse that has jammed airports and disrupted travel, just as TSA workers faced another missed paycheck Friday.

(Image credit: Noah K. Murray)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
item.description: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.
item.content:encoded:
The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

(Image credit: Leon Neal)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: As TSA agents miss another paycheck, what's happening at airports with private security?
item.description: At 20 airports around the U.S., security screeners are getting paid as usual despite the ongoing DHS shutdown — because they're private contractors. Will more airports look at privatizing security?
item.content:encoded: At 20 airports around the U.S., security screeners are getting paid as usual despite the ongoing DHS shutdown — because they're private contractors. Will more airports look at privatizing security?
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Trump has deployed ICE agents to the nation's airports. What's their role?
item.description: The president says ICE agents are being stationed at airports to help reduce long wait times. Here's a look at what they're authorized to do.
item.content:encoded:
The president says ICE agents are being stationed at airports to help reduce long wait times. Here's a look at what they're authorized to do.

(Image credit: Nam Y. Huh)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: DOJ admits ICE courthouse arrests relied on erroneous information
item.description: Hundreds of immigrants have been arrested at immigration courthouses. It is unclear whether the federal government's admission could lead to some of those arrests being overturned.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of immigrants have been arrested at immigration courthouses. It is unclear whether the federal government's admission could lead to some of those arrests being overturned.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: America's first AI-fueled war is unfolding right now in Iran. This is how we got here
item.description: Bloomberg journalist Katrina Manson discusses the Pentagon's secretive campaign to build America's AI warfare capabilities and the obsessive Marine colonel behind it. Her new book is Project Maven.
item.content:encoded: Bloomberg journalist Katrina Manson discusses the Pentagon's secretive campaign to build America's AI warfare capabilities and the obsessive Marine colonel behind it. Her new book is Project Maven.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Suit asks court to force Trump administration to use 'The Kennedy Center' name
item.description: The motion is part of a lawsuit challenging President Trump and the Center's board, who now refer to the complex as "The Trump Kennedy Center."
item.content:encoded:
The motion is part of a lawsuit challenging President Trump and the Center's board, who now refer to the complex as "The Trump Kennedy Center."

(Image credit: Jim Watson)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas
